World Cup opening ceremony and Mexico vs South Africa LIVE: 2026 tournament kicks off after Gianni Infantino tells World Cup critics to 'CHILL'

By LUKE POWER, SPORTS REPORTER and HARRY BAMFORTH, LIVE SPORTS COVERAGE REPORTER and OLIVER HOLT, CHIEF SPORTS WRITER and JEFF POWELL MBE, SPORTS WRITER AND BOXING COLUMNIST and CHARLOTTE DALY, MULTIMEDIA CONTENT REPORTER

Follow Daily Mail Sport's live blog for the latest score, team news and updates as Mexico vs South Africa kicks off the 2026 World Cup following the opening ceremony. 

15:17

Does Infantino regret the USA being a co-host?

By Luke Power

Infantino was asked in his press conference if he regrets the USA being a co-host of this tournament, what with all the... strife.

The smooth-headed FIFA suit said: 'No, I don't regret anything. I have been organising events for the last 30 years. I am used to dealing with issues.

'The press conference I made you all remember that. The issues there were different, probably bigger. I was feeling I had to give a voice to all those unheard. There are issues; it's normal for an event of this magnitude.

'I would always hope there are no issues, but hey, that's what we have to deal with. Some come from the United States, some from Canada, some from Mexico. We deal with all of them and we have the same positive spirit.

'Hopefully, we can solve all of them. Probably not, but we still do our best.'

15:08

The atmosphere builds (and so does the security presence!)

By Luke Power

More and more supporters are trickling towards the vicinity of the Estadio Azteca. Never mind that we're still five hours away from kick-off!

One of the fans is looking particularly spooky in a costume which would look more at home in the annual Dia de los Muertos (Day of the Dead) festival in November.

As more people stream towards the stadium, so the security detail burgeons.

15:02

YOUR opinion on Gianni Infantino

By Luke Power

Thanks to those of you who have been writing in from around the world. So far we've had comments from the United Kingdom and Canada. Let's get more of the earth covered!

One of you, shose opinion is aptly named 'Opinions and Views', says: 'The man is a complete and utter fool.'

I can only hope you're referring to FIFA president Gianni Infantino and not me...

14:59

The best World Cup art goes to...

By Luke Power

Bangladesh!

A great effort by artists in Tikatulia area of Old Dhaka, Bangladesh, with their alley way paintings.

I'm not entirely sure Erling Haaland will be satisfied with his depiction but it's a 10/10 for effort.

14:54

The Daily Mail's World Cup A-Z

By Luke Power

Time for one of those offbeat, slightly unnecessary, totally obligatory guides to the World Cup.

For every letter of the alphabet, we've got you covered. Read this and you'll probably learn a few interesting tidbits. That's worth something, right?

Explore more

2026 WORLD CUP A-Z: Our alternative guide to greatest show on earth

Here, Daily Mail Sport is keen to provide one handy guide to the beauty and the madness awaiting fans at home and abroad alike, with a run-through from A to Z.

14:50

Rejected Somalian referee given top European game

By Luke Power

Somalian referee Omar Artan, who was denied entry to the United States due to his 'association with suspected members of terror organisations', has been given a top European game.

The 34-year-old will take charge of the UEFA Super Cup clash between Paris Saint-Germain and Aston Villa on August 12.

Africa's top referee was welcomed home as a hero in Mogadishu yesterday.

WORLD CUP DAILY: 'Omar Artan has already made a promise'

14:42

Football fever hits Mexico City

By Luke Power

Mexico City is officially in the grip of football. No surprise, perhaps, for a city with three major clubs.

Some shameless sod has been flogging Lionel Messi shirts, which feels a bit of a kick in the teeth if the vendor is a local.

Absolutely no idea what the installations are meant to be in the second image and to be honest I find them a bit creepy. Stop reaching your arms out, flat-faced statue. Don't touch me!

14:37

Weather check

By Luke Power

The temperature for kick-off should be around 24 degrees celcius, nothing Mexico and South Africa won't be able to handle.

There is the threat of a thunderstorm this afternoon, which could make things interesting in terms of a potential postponement, but we'll know nearer the time.

A game will be temporarily called off if lightning is detected within an eight-mile radius of a stadium, and then half an hour has to pass after the final strike for the game to be resumed.

14:30

Is this the BEST World Cup song?

By Luke Power

I can't claim to have listened to them all but I think you'll struggle to find a better national theme tune than this.

Bosnia and Herzegovina have absolutely smashed it out of the park with their suspiciously Eurovision-style entry.

'I am from Bosnia, take me to America,

I really want to see Statue of Liberty

I can no longer wait, take me to United States

Take me to Golden Gate, I will assimilate!'

Cheesy, overenthusiastic, but what a tune!

14:26

How are YOU watching the World Cup?

By Luke Power

Let's get a bit of engagement going in the comments.

Are you travelling to the World Cup or staying curled up on the sofa? Are you going out today to watch the opening ceremony?

Let us know how you're watching the tournament and, if you have travelled, what your experiences have been like so far.

Crucially, if you're a fan anywhere in the world, how do you think your team will get on?

14:21

Your ultimate guide to the World Cup

By Luke Power

Feeling a little bit overwhelmed by the information crashing at you from 360 different angles?

Not really a fan of football, but just tune in for the best bits? (No judgement.)

Caught a nasty case of World Cup fever and just want to soak up as much content as possible?

Whoever you are, hopefully you fall into one of those three camps. If that's you, you'll find our mega World Cup guide a useful companion to navigate this opening day and get your head around what's going on. It's one of many detailed pieces we've put together for this World Cup.

Explore more

The World Cup starts TODAY! Here's everything you need to know

Forty-eight teams are set to take centre stage in the United States, Canada and Mexico - from the tiny Caribbean island of Curacao, with a population of 150,000, to five-time world champions Brazil.

14:18

Mexican president gifts her ticket to indigenous athlete

By Luke Power

A nice gesture from Mexico's president.

Claudia Sheinbaum has given her ticket to the opening match to Yolett Cervantes Cuaquehua, a 21-year-old football player.

Maybe heart-warming things can happen at this World Cup!
